faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Harlem home?
faqs.answer: Signs include piles of sawdust (frass) near wooden structures, rustling sounds in walls or ceilings, and large black ants inside your home, especially near windows or moisture sources. Carpenter ants are active at night and often trail along baseboards. If you see winged ants indoors during spring, you likely have an established colony.

faqs.question: What does carpenter ant control cost in Harlem?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Harlem typically ranges from $350 to $2,000, depending on the size of your property, severity of infestation, and number of colonies present. Brownstones and multi-family buildings may require more extensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Can carpenter ants damage my Harlem brownstone?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants excavate wood to build nests, which can seriously damage wooden beams, joists, window frames, and structural elements common in Harlem's historic buildings. Unlike termites, they don't eat wood, but the galleries they create weaken structures over time. Early detection and professional treatment are essential to prevent costly repairs.

faqs.question: How quickly can you treat carpenter ants in Harlem?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Harlem. Most treatments are completed in one visit, though severe infestations may require follow-up applications. Our technicians will locate nests, apply targeted treatments, and create a prevention plan. You'll typically see significant reduction within 48-72 hours, with complete elimination within 1-2 weeks.
